title: ranunculus acris f. oreodoxa
intro: Ranunculus acris f. oreodoxa, also known as meadow buttercup, is a subspecies of the buttercup plant that grows in wet meadows and along streams. The plant has been used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations, wounds, and minor burns. Consuming Ranunculus acris f. oreodoxa is said to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving rheumatic pain, and acting as a diuretic. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and leaves can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions to harness its medicinal properties.
